front: Concept: infants aged 6-18 months first recognize their reflection, forming the ego through apperception; the ego is a meconnaissance (misrecognition)
work: The Mirror Stage and Ego Formation

front: Concept: Lacan's three registers — the order of images and ego, the order of language and law, and that which resists symbolization — portrayed as a Borromean Knot
work: The Three Orders: Imaginary, Symbolic, and Real

front: Concept: the object-cause of desire, related to 'surplus jouissance' (paralleling Marx's surplus value)
work: Objet Petit a, Jouissance, and Mathemes

front: Concept: pseudo-mathematical symbols like $ <> a (for 'fantasy') used to formalize psychoanalytic concepts; this thinker also identified four discourses (master, university, analyst, hysteric)
work: Objet Petit a, Jouissance, and Mathemes

front: Work: a seminar arguing that the title document from a Poe story is a signifier of the unknown whose presence-absence drives the plot
Philosopher: Laura Mulvey developed 'the male gaze' from this thinker's concept of the 'gaze,' which this thinker illustrated using the anamorphic skull in Holbein's The Ambassadors
Philosopher: Slavoj Zizek frequently applies this thinker to political questions, including in The Pervert's Guide to Cinema and The Sublime Object of Ideology
Philosopher: was stripped of IPA recognition for variable-length therapy sessions; took his reading of Hegel from Alexander Kojeve
Philosopher: Felix Guattari developed schizoanalysis to improve on this thinker's psychoanalytic work
